name: python3-celery
title: python3-celery
summary: async task/job queue based on message passing (Python3 version)
description:
async task/job queue based on message passing (Python3 version)
Celery is an open source asynchronous task queue/job queue based on
distributed message passing. It is focused on real-time operation,
but supports scheduling as well.
.
The execution units, called tasks, are executed concurrently on one
or more worker nodes. Tasks can execute asynchronously (in the
background) or synchronously (wait until ready).
.
Celery is written in Python, but the protocol can be implemented
in any language. It can also operate with other languages using
webhooks.
.
The recommended message broker is RabbitMQ, but limited support for Redis,
Beanstalk, MongoDB, CouchDB, and databases (using SQLAlchemy or the Django
ORM) is also available. Celery is easy to integrate with Django, using the
python-django-celery package.
.
This package contains the Python 3 version of the library.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-celery because async task/job queue based on message passing (Python3 version).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-celery as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-celery with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-celery --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
